message.content:
Test file: src/claim-office.spec.ts
Failing test: "highly enchanted sword (enchantment 5) adds 30% surcharge (premium = 145G)"
Implementation file: src/claim-office.ts

Add: items with enchantment >= 5 add 30% surcharge of their base premium. Add this to the existing item surcharge logic alongside cursed.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, proceed to Refactor after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implement the minimal code needed to make it pass.
Now I'll implement the minimal change to add the high-enchantment surcharge alongside the cursed surcharge.

content.text:
All 14 tests pass (the new high-enchantment test plus the previously passing 13).

Verification: For the failing test, sword with enchantment 5: base = 100, high-ench surcharge = 30, item surcharges = 30, first insurance = 10, fee = 5. Total = 100 + 30 + 10 + 5 = 145. Matches expected.

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Added HIGH_ENCHANTMENT_SURCHARGE_RATE (0.3) and HIGH_ENCHANTMENT_THRESHOLD (5) constants, extended Item type with optional enchantment, and updated itemSurcharge to add 30% surcharge of base premium when enchantment >= 5, alongside the existing cursed surcharge.
- Result: All 14 active tests pass (29 still todo).
- Approach: Minimal addition — the surcharge logic mirrors the existing cursed pattern, summing both surcharges so they can stack naturally when needed. Used base * rate (where base is the item's base premium) directly per the spec.

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
